ucs.title: [LLM] BackdoorDiplomacy Turian DLL search-order hijack via co-planted signed AV/security vendor binary
ucs.description: Hunts BackdoorDiplomacy's signature Turian/Quarian deployment pattern: a legitimately-signed third-party binary (commonly McAfee Mcods.exe, Sophos scrobserver.exe, Logitech KHALMNPR.exe) is dropped to a non-standard path (ProgramData, Public, ad-hoc folders) alongside a malicious side-loaded DLL (e.g. mcvsocfg.dll, ssMUIDLL.dll). ESET documented this exact tradecraft for the actor (Cluster25/ESET 2021 'BackdoorDiplomacy' report).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("Mcods.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","scrobserver.exe","KHALMNPR.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","AppLaunch.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe") AND NOT (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Program Files\\*","*\\Program Files (x86)\\*","*\\Windows\\*")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host process_path [ | tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_name) as sideloaded_dll from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created Filesystem.file_name IN ("mcvsocfg.dll","mcutil.dll","ssMUIDLL.dll","version.dll","libcurl.dll","vftrace.dll","wsc.dll") by host Filesystem.file_path | rename Filesystem.file_path as process_path Filesystem.file_name as sideloaded_dll | eval process_path=replace(process_path,"\\\\[^\\\\]+$","") ] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// BackdoorDiplomacy / Turian side-loading — legit signed binary + malicious sibling DLL
let _abused_carriers = dynamic(["mcods.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","scrobserver.exe","khalmnpr.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","applaunch.exe","googleupdate.exe"]);
let _hijack_dlls = dynamic(["mcvsocfg.dll","mcutil.dll","ssmuidll.dll","version.dll","libcurl.dll","vftrace.dll","wsc.dll","goopdate.dll"]);
let _legit_paths = dynamic([@"c:\program files\", @"c:\program files (x86)\", @"c:\windows\"]);
let Carriers = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName in~ (_abused_carriers)
    | where not(tolower(FolderPath) has_any (_legit_paths))
    | project CarrierTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              CarrierFile=FileName, CarrierPath=FolderPath, CarrierSHA256=SHA256, CarrierCmd=ProcessCommandLine,
              CarrierDir = strcat(tostring(split(FolderPath, FileName)[0]));
let SideLoadDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where FileName in~ (_hijack_dlls)
    | where not(tolower(FolderPath) has_any (_legit_paths))
    | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DropDLL=FileName, DropPath=FolderPath, DropSHA256=SHA256,
              DropDir = strcat(tostring(split(FolderPath, FileName)[0])),
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
Carriers
| join kind=inner SideLoadDrops on DeviceId
| where tolower(CarrierDir) == tolower(DropDir)        // co-located in same dir
| where DropTime between (CarrierTime - 1d .. CarrierTime + 1h)
| project CarrierTime, DropTime, DeviceName, AccountName,
          CarrierFile, CarrierPath, CarrierSHA256,
          DropDLL, DropSHA256, DropPath,
          DropperProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName, DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by CarrierTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BackdoorDiplomacy / Turian side-loading — legit signed binary + malicious sibling DLL
let _abused_carriers = dynamic(["mcods.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","scrobserver.exe","khalmnpr.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","applaunch.exe","googleupdate.exe"]);
let _hijack_dlls = dynamic(["mcvsocfg.dll","mcutil.dll","ssmuidll.dll","version.dll","libcurl.dll","vftrace.dll","wsc.dll","goopdate.dll"]);
let _legit_paths = dynamic([@"c:\program files\", @"c:\program files (x86)\", @"c:\windows\"]);
let Carriers = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend ImageLower = tolower(NewProcessName)
    | where ImageLower has_any (_abused_carriers)
    | where not(ImageLower has_any (_legit_paths))
    | extend CarrierFile = tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1])
    | extend CarrierDir = substring(NewProcessName, 0, strlen(NewProcessName) - strlen(CarrierFile))
    | project CarrierTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, CarrierFile, CarrierDir, CarrierCmd=CommandLine;
let SideLoadDrops = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | extend DllLower = tolower(TargetFileName)
    | where DllLower has_any (_hijack_dlls)
    | extend DropDir = substring(TargetFilePath, 0, strlen(TargetFilePath) - strlen(TargetFileName))
    | where not(tolower(DropDir) has_any (_legit_paths))
    | project DropTime=TimeGenerated, Computer=DvcHostname, DropDLL=TargetFileName, DropDir, DropSHA256=TargetFileSHA256,
              Dropper=ActingProcessName, DropperCmd=ActingProcessCommandLine;
Carriers
| join kind=inner SideLoadDrops on Computer
| where tolower(CarrierDir) == tolower(DropDir)
| where DropTime between (CarrierTime - 1d .. CarrierTime + 1h)
| project CarrierTime, DropTime, Computer, Account, CarrierFile, CarrierDir,
          DropDLL, DropSHA256, Dropper, DropperCmd
| order by CarrierTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: BackdoorDiplomacy Turian Side-Loading Carrier Binary From Non-Standard Path
id: 8c1d9b41-4c2f-4a4d-a4c5-bd0d10b2a311
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of a legitimately-signed third-party binary (McAfee Mcods, Sophos scrobserver, Logitech KHALMNPR, etc.) that BackdoorDiplomacy is known to abuse for DLL search-order hijacking, when run from a non-standard install path.
references:
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2021/06/10/backdoordiplomacy-upgrading-quarian-turian/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0135/
author: SOC Detection Eng
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.001
  - attack.t1036.005
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\Mcods.exe'
      - '\mcoemcpy.exe'
      - '\scrobserver.exe'
      - '\KHALMNPR.exe'
      - '\wsc_proxy.exe'
      - '\AppLaunch.exe'
      - '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
  filter_legit_paths:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Program Files\'
      - '\Program Files (x86)\'
      - '\Windows\'
  condition: selection_image and not filter_legit_paths
falsepositives:
  - Portable / repackaged installers run from custom directories
level: high
ucs.rationale: ESET's June-2021 report on BackdoorDiplomacy explicitly named these signed-vendor carriers and the DLL pairs they hijack to load Turian. Random APTs that side-load typically pick different vendor pairs (LuminousMoth → MS Word/MFC, Mustang Panda → Adobe ARM); the specific carrier+DLL+co-located-path combination here is BackdoorDiplomacy's fingerprint, not a generic side-load template.

ucs.title: [LLM] BackdoorDiplomacy IIS / Exchange webshell post-exploitation: w3wp.exe → recon + tunnel staging
ucs.description: Hunts BackdoorDiplomacy's well-documented intrusion chain on internet-facing servers — exploitation of public-facing apps (F5 BIG-IP CVE-2020-5902, Microsoft Exchange ProxyShell, Plesk) followed by China Chopper-style webshell drop, then w3wp.exe spawning recon binaries and dropping their EarthWorm/reGeorg tunneler. ESET observed this chain repeatedly against Ministries of Foreign Affairs and telecoms.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines dc(Processes.process_name) as distinct_children values(Processes.process_name) as children from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat*.exe","nginx.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","quser.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","schtasks.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where distinct_children >= 2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// BackdoorDiplomacy webshell footprint — IIS/Exchange w3wp spawning hands-on-keyboard recon
let RecentDays = 7d;
let ReconBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","quser.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","schtasks.exe"]);
let WebHostParents = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","nginx.exe","php-cgi.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(RecentDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (WebHostParents)
| where FileName in~ (ReconBins)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// noise filter — IIS app-pool identities running benign single-shot health checks
| where not(InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("HealthMailbox","MSExchangeHM"))
| summarize ChildCount = dcount(FileName),
            DistinctChildren = make_set(FileName, 25),
            SampleCmds = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 25),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            ParentCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
            AppPoolUser = any(InitiatingProcessAccountName)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
| where ChildCount >= 2          // ≥2 distinct LOLBins from the same w3wp in 1h = hands-on-keyboard, not single ASP.NET callout
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BackdoorDiplomacy webshell footprint — IIS/Exchange w3wp spawning recon LOLBins
let RecentDays = 7d;
let ReconBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","quser.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","schtasks.exe"]);
let WebHostParents = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","nginx.exe","php-cgi.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(RecentDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ChildName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| extend ParentName = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ParentName in (WebHostParents)
| where ChildName in (ReconBins)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where not(CommandLine has_any ("HealthMailbox","MSExchangeHM"))
| summarize ChildCount = dcount(ChildName),
            DistinctChildren = make_set(ChildName, 25),
            SampleCmds = make_set(CommandLine, 25),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            AppPoolUser = any(SubjectUserName)
            by Computer, ParentName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where ChildCount >= 2
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Webserver Process Spawning Recon LOLBin (Webshell Activity — BackdoorDiplomacy / China Chopper)
id: 4d2a7f10-9c1c-4d1d-8cb6-29a9f88c7710
status: experimental
description: Detects an IIS / Exchange / Tomcat / Nginx / PHP-CGI worker process spawning recon or download LOLBins, consistent with webshell-driven post-exploitation as observed for BackdoorDiplomacy after exploiting public-facing servers (Exchange ProxyShell, F5 BIG-IP CVE-2020-5902, Plesk).
references:
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2021/06/10/backdoordiplomacy-upgrading-quarian-turian/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0135/
author: SOC Detection Eng
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\tomcat.exe'
      - '\tomcat9.exe'
      - '\nginx.exe'
      - '\php-cgi.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\sc.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
  filter_health:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'HealthMailbox'
      - 'MSExchangeHM'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_health
falsepositives:
  - Custom monitoring scripts deployed via the web tier
  - Legacy app deployment pipelines invoking IIS reset / WMI from inside w3wp
level: high
ucs.rationale: BackdoorDiplomacy's published kill chain (ESET 2021, multiple Mandiant/Cluster25 follow-ups) is virtually always: T1190 against an internet-facing server → China Chopper webshell on IIS/Exchange → w3wp-spawned recon → drop EarthWorm/reGeorg + Turian. Most APTs avoid noisy multi-LOLBin spawns from w3wp; BackdoorDiplomacy operators have repeatedly burned themselves on this exact sequence against MFA
